text: Git branching is an essential part of modern version control. A common workflow involves creating a feature branch off the main codebase. This allows you to develop new features or fix bugs in isolation, without affecting the stable code. Once your work is complete, you can create a pull request to propose your changes. This triggers a discussion among teammates, who can review the code, suggest improvements, and run automated tests. After approval, the changes are merged into the main branch. This structured workflow helps teams collaborate efficiently, maintain clean project history, and reduce conflicts.
